description: Clear-signing registry of dangerous Anchor instructions matched by their 8-byte sha256(global:<name>)[0..8] discriminator. A 'program' of '*' matches any program carrying that discriminator (discriminator-only match). A specific base58 program id narrows the match to that program. Severity REJECT = admin/ownership transfer that enables immediate full control loss; HOLD = significant protocol-level danger requiring human review.

entries.name: update_admin
entries.label: Anchor: update_admin (admin transfer)
entries.mapsToLoss: Transfers the admin role of a protocol to the new address specified in the instruction. An attacker gaining admin can drain protocol funds, modify parameters, or rug users instantly.

entries.name: set_admin
entries.label: Anchor: set_admin (admin assignment)
entries.mapsToLoss: Assigns or replaces the admin authority of an Anchor program. Equivalent to update_admin; full protocol control transfers to the new admin immediately.

entries.name: transfer_admin
entries.label: Anchor: transfer_admin (admin transfer)
entries.mapsToLoss: Initiates or completes an admin authority transfer. Once the new admin accepts, they gain full control over the protocol.

entries.name: set_owner
entries.label: Anchor: set_owner (ownership assignment)
entries.mapsToLoss: Replaces the owner of an Anchor-managed account or protocol. The new owner can withdraw funds, modify state, or reassign further authority.

entries.name: transfer_ownership
entries.label: Anchor: transfer_ownership (ownership transfer)
entries.mapsToLoss: Transfers ownership of the protocol or a key account to a new address. Enables complete takeover by the recipient.

entries.name: set_authority
entries.label: Anchor: set_authority (authority assignment)
entries.mapsToLoss: Sets a new authority on an Anchor-managed resource. Depending on the authority type, enables withdrawals, upgrades, or parameter control by the attacker.

entries.name: update_authority
entries.label: Anchor: update_authority (authority update)
entries.mapsToLoss: Replaces an existing authority. Same danger surface as set_authority.

entries.name: set_upgrade_authority
entries.label: Anchor: set_upgrade_authority (upgrade authority transfer)
entries.mapsToLoss: Transfers the upgrade authority of a program account. The new authority can deploy arbitrary bytecode, enabling an instant rug.

entries.name: update_whitelist
entries.label: Anchor: update_whitelist (whitelist modification)
entries.mapsToLoss: Modifies an on-chain whitelist controlling which addresses may interact with the protocol. An attacker can add themselves or remove legitimate actors.

entries.name: add_collateral
entries.label: Anchor: add_collateral (collateral modification)
entries.mapsToLoss: Adds or modifies collateral configuration in a lending or perpetuals protocol. Misconfiguration enables undercollateralized borrowing or oracle manipulation attacks.

entries.name: update_oracle
entries.label: Anchor: update_oracle (oracle configuration change)
entries.mapsToLoss: Replaces or reconfigures a price oracle. A manipulated oracle enables draining a protocol via inflated/deflated prices.
